title: Fluffy Greek Filo Pilaf
description: A nutty, fluffy, and delicious Greek dish perfect for brunch, featuring melted butter and sautéed ingredients. This recipe is also diabetic-friendly due to its moderate carb content and use of healthy fats.
instructions: In a large skillet, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked onion and garlic mixture, chicken broth, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Layer the filo dough in a large baking dish, brushing each layer with melted butter. Repeat this process until all the filo dough is used, ending with a layer of butter on top.
Pour the onion and garlic mixture over the filo dough, making sure to cover the entire surface.
Sprinkle the crumbly feta cheese and chopped parsley over the top of the pilaf.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
